jokes.text: Don't worry. It's too early for the creative types, and I sent my assistant to a nonexistent Italian bakery out in Queens.
jokes.setup: Jack reassuring about not being seen
jokes.punchline: He deliberately sent his assistant on a wild goose chase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - casual corporate cruelty delivered matter-of-factly. Shows his manipulative nature while serving plot function.

jokes.text: I can't go back there without sfogliatelle!
jokes.setup: Assistant's response to being sent to nonexistent bakery
jokes.punchline: His desperate commitment to finding specific Italian pastries
jokes.explanation: Quick character beat showing the assistant's earnestness. The specific Italian pastry name adds authenticity to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Take off your pants suit.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'pantsuit' - both clothing item and instruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that's both sexy and grammatically clever. Maximum economy - three words doing double duty.

jokes.text: Oh! Workplace!
jokes.punchline: Jack's panicked attempt to make their interaction seem professional
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective panic response. The single word 'workplace' as an explanation is absurd.

jokes.text: I gave up caffeine, so I've been going to bed at 5:30.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly early bedtime as result of giving up caffeine
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz self-deprecation about her unhealthy lifestyle changes. Relatable but taken to absurd extreme.

jokes.text: Lakeesha Gutierrez-Arafat
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake name combining multiple ethnicities
jokes.explanation: Absurd fake name that's obviously made up. Shows Jack's terrible improvisation skills under pressure.

jokes.text: It's actually a rape whistle but the whistle part fell off, and I just liked how it looked so I kept it.
jokes.punchline: Dark revelation about broken safety device worn as jewelry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that subverts expectations completely. Takes innocent compliment to very dark place while revealing character's practicality.

jokes.text: 'It' being business. Of course. I call the movie 'Risky Business' 'Risky It.' Because 'it' means business.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to play along with the fake business meeting
jokes.punchline: Convoluted wordplay about 'Risky Business' title
jokes.explanation: Typical Liz overthinking a simple situation, making it more awkward. The wordplay is labored but that's the point - shows her social awkwardness.

jokes.text: I went to Harvard. I did stand-up there this weekend.
jokes.setup: Accused of not going to Harvard
jokes.punchline: Reveals he just performed there, not studied
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that's perfectly in character for Tracy's ego and loose relationship with truth.

jokes.text: Well, I got a squeezer from an Indian girl on a bunk bed, so I think I got the full Harvard experience.
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual reference as proof of college experience
jokes.explanation: Typical Tracy inappropriate oversharing. Gets laugh but relies on crude humor without much craft.

jokes.text: Oh, that word bums me out unless it's between the words 'meat' and 'pizza.'
jokes.punchline: Liz only likes 'lovers' in 'meat lovers pizza'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Liz's food obsession and discomfort with romance. Great character integration.

jokes.text: This corporation has a very strict 'bros before hos' policy.
jokes.punchline: Corporate policy stated in crude fratboy terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of corporate speak with fratboy language. Highly quotable and perfectly satirizes corporate culture.

jokes.text: Okay, if I can't say 'lovers,' you can't say 'feelings.'
jokes.setup: Liz asking about his feelings for C.C.
jokes.punchline: Jack's equal revulsion to emotional vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - his emotional unavailability expressed as linguistic squeamishness. Great callback to earlier 'lovers' complaint.

jokes.text: And try not to dress like a small-town lesbian.
jokes.setup: Jack asking Liz to dinner party
jokes.punchline: Insult about Liz's fashion sense
jokes.explanation: Typical Jack casual insult. Specific and character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Well, Lemon, that was a good chat. Good luck with that alopecia problem of yours.
jokes.punchline: Fake concern about hair loss problem
jokes.explanation: Jack's casual cruelty disguised as concern. The medical term 'alopecia' makes it funnier than just saying 'baldness.'

jokes.text: Oh, come on! I don't have that. Very hairy.
jokes.punchline: Liz's defensive overcompensation
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz overreaction to Jack's needling. The 'very hairy' protest makes it worse.

jokes.text: Do I? I don't.
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction of herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - agreeing then immediately admitting she wasn't listening. Great economy.

jokes.text: Best Actress in a Movie Based on a Musical Based on a Movie!
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and circular award category
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of overly specific award categories. The circular nature (movie→musical→movie) is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Like where are the french fries I did not ask for? You guys need to anticipate me.
jokes.punchline: Demanding things he didn't ask for
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy entitlement and ego. The impossible standard of anticipating unspoken desires is classic celebrity behavior.

jokes.text: Oh, no. I talked to him last time when he wanted to change his name to 'Wise Greasy Bastard.'
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous ridiculous name change request
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the ongoing absurdity of Tracy's demands. 'Wise Greasy Bastard' is memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: Oh, monsters, why did I create you?
jokes.setup: Dealing with Tracy and Jenna's problems
jokes.punchline: Liz as Frankenstein to her monster stars
jokes.explanation: Nice meta joke about Liz's role as show creator dealing with difficult talent. Literary reference adds class.

jokes.text: Boy, I hope we rescue the Starfish King on this adventure. It'll be a slam dunk!
jokes.punchline: Shaq's basketball pun in inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of celebrity animated movies where stars phone in performances with inappropriate catchphrases.

jokes.text: Would you call what we did last night 'sex'?
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual question mixed into children's dialogue
jokes.explanation: Shock humor that works because of the jarring context shift from children's movie to adult content.

jokes.text: A million dollars, a yellow Bentley, and nothing.
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about not getting anything
jokes.punchline: Lists expensive rewards then says 'nothing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy entitlement - literally getting everything but feeling empty without awards recognition. Great character work.

jokes.text: Maybe I should just give up and try this crystal meth I've been hearing so much about.
jokes.setup: Tracy's despair over lack of awards
jokes.punchline: Casual consideration of hard drugs as solution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works because of Tracy's casual tone and celebrity disconnect from reality.

jokes.text: Pacific Rim Emmys.
jokes.punchline: Fake regional Emmy variant
jokes.explanation: Good fake award name that sounds plausible but is obviously made up. Sets up the episode's central deception.

jokes.text: I spent nine months in Japan shooting 'Samurai, I-Am-Urai.'
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun movie title
jokes.explanation: Perfectly terrible Hollywood pun title that Tracy would absolutely star in. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: It's when you run up on a lady on the streets and pull her tube top down while your friend videotapes it. It's considered a crime here. Thanks a lot, Puritans.
jokes.punchline: Describing sexual assault as cultural difference, blaming Puritans
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about sexual assault that lands because of Tracy's obliviousness and the 'Puritans' misdirection. Controversial but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I'm just kidding. I know he doesn't care what humans do.
jokes.setup: Tracy not wanting to disappoint Godzilla
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging Godzilla's indifference to human affairs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic - Tracy treating Godzilla as real celebrity while acknowledging his theoretical indifference. Perfect character beat.

jokes.text: Props has an old basketball trophy we could solder some wings onto.
jokes.punchline: Hilariously makeshift Emmy construction
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup showing the pathetic lengths they'll go to. The specific 'solder wings' detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're my rock. It is amazing how grounded I've been able to stay despite my célébrité.
jokes.setup: Liz calling Jenna the sane one
jokes.punchline: Jenna using French for 'celebrity' while claiming to be grounded
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - claims humility while being pretentious. The French pronunciation of 'celebrity' is perfectly obnoxious.

jokes.text: What's up, dude? I went to Harvard.
jokes.punchline: Casual repetition of false claim
jokes.explanation: Running gag that works through repetition and Tracy's commitment to the lie. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I lived in Lowell House and was a member of the Signet Society.
jokes.punchline: Kenneth revealing actual Harvard credentials
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character revelation - innocent Kenneth has Ivy League pedigree while Tracy lies about it. Perfect misdirection and character depth.

jokes.text: Your lame thing is on his pants.
jokes.punchline: Tracy's weak comeback about the pin
jokes.explanation: Tracy's defensive reaction shows he's been caught in his lie. The weakness of the comeback is the joke.

jokes.text: Perfect. Just when I get back from Maiden Voyage, Newark's first offshore gentlemen's barge.
jokes.punchline: Specific ridiculous strip club boat concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd concept - offshore strip club to avoid regulations, perfectly specific to Newark. Great Tracy-level ridiculousness.

jokes.text: Then you can check out my award. Ha ha ha! Banter!
jokes.punchline: Announcing 'Banter!' like it's a category
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about Tracy labeling his own attempted humor. Shows his lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: No, don't blame him. You're the one who rewards his bad behavior. You give him anything he wants.
jokes.punchline: Josh pointing out enabling pattern
jokes.explanation: Straight-man observation about the show's central dynamic. More plot advancement than pure comedy.

jokes.text: We had a longer invite list, but we thought for the sake of discretion... We had to eliminate anybody with any influence or connections or stature, so that pretty much left you.
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about Liz's lack of importance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack cruelty disguised as explanation. The systematic elimination logic makes the insult more devastating.

jokes.text: Then we added Kenneth so you would have somebody to talk to.
jokes.punchline: Kenneth as Liz's social equal
jokes.explanation: Follow-up insult that compounds the first one. Implies Liz and Kenneth are on same social level.

jokes.text: What's your favorite pizza topping? Mine's plain, but I like others.
jokes.punchline: Incredibly bland small talk preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocent awkwardness. His idea of sophisticated dinner conversation is painfully mundane.

jokes.text: Shelley Long, you're truly an inspiration to me!
jokes.punchline: Random reference to 80s actress
jokes.explanation: Random celebrity reference that's perfectly Tracy. Shelley Long is specific enough to be funny without explanation.

jokes.text: wanizame atakku! Shark attack!
jokes.punchline: Calls back to sharking discussion from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier inappropriate cultural reference. Shows Tracy's continued poor judgment.

jokes.text: But I did hear the janitor saying your boobs looked good.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to cheer up Jenna
jokes.punchline: Pathetically inappropriate consolation prize
jokes.explanation: Liz's desperate attempt to find any positive recognition for Jenna. Appropriately pathetic and awkward.

jokes.text: Aah! The squirrel! It's not afraid of people!
jokes.setup: Jenna locking herself in dressing room
jokes.punchline: Confronted by fearless squirrel
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier squirrel setup. The 'not afraid of people' detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It's like we share a brain.
jokes.punchline: Claiming deep connection over nonsense
jokes.explanation: Good irony about couple who think random references equal deep connection. Satirizes pretentious relationships.

jokes.text: You can't pass, you hillbilly!
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to pass on charades
jokes.punchline: C.C.'s aggressive response to innocent Kenneth
jokes.explanation: Shows C.C.'s competitive nature turning ugly. The 'hillbilly' insult reveals her elitist side.

jokes.text: I'm-a the plumber. I'm here-a to fix the washing machine.
jokes.punchline: Jack's terrible Italian accent/disguise
jokes.explanation: Classic broad comedy disguise. Jack's corporate dignity undercut by silly accent.

jokes.text: I ate a 3 Musketeers bar for breakfast this morning, and this bra is held together with tape.
jokes.punchline: Liz's evidence of her incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz self-awareness about her disaster of a personal life. Specific details make it funnier and more pathetic.

jokes.text: Oh, God, it was my birthday yesterday.
jokes.punchline: Sudden realization of her own forgotten birthday
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Liz so focused on others' problems she forgot her own birthday. Perfect economy and sadness.

jokes.text: He didn't leave. He's a submarine commander running silent.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate military explanation for abandonment
jokes.explanation: Great defense mechanism - Frank's elaborate fantasy about deadbeat dad. 'Running silent' is perfect submarine terminology.

jokes.text: $5 million each? That's NBA sexual-assault money.
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to sports scandal payouts
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's both specific and sadly accurate about celebrity scandal payouts. Great corporate insider perspective.

jokes.text: It's like seeing a dog wearing clothes.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment comparing women to dressed animals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack casual sexism disguised as compliment. The specific comparison is memorably offensive yet character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I should never have listened to a woman who tapes her bra together.
jokes.punchline: References Liz's earlier tape bra admission
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows C.C. was listening and judges Liz harshly. Pays off earlier setup well.

jokes.text: She told an NBC tour that she was going to get them all pregnant.
jokes.punchline: Threatening tourists with pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of Jenna's diva behavior. The threat of pregnancy to random tourists is perfectly insane.

jokes.text: And your name is probably something like Melissa.
jokes.punchline: Dismissively guessing Liz's name wrong
jokes.explanation: Good establishment of obnoxious entourage member. The specific wrong name guess adds to the dismissiveness.

jokes.text: Oh, Melissa? Your face is on the phone. Soccer practice is over and you need to pick it up.
jokes.punchline: Insulting her as suburban mom with generic responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Continues the Melissa mockery with specific suburban mom stereotypes. 'Your face is on the phone' is a weird insult.

jokes.text: How do you and Mary Matalin make it work? She's a brilliant, patriotic Republican strategist, and you, let's face it, are a pinko nutjob.
jokes.setup: Jack asking Carville for relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Insulting Carville while asking for help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack tactlessness - insulting someone while asking for favor. The political description is perfectly partisan.

jokes.text: Even Desert Storm.
jokes.setup: Saying their love can weather any storm
jokes.punchline: References specific military conflict
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'storm' metaphor, referencing actual historical event. Great economy and Carville would absolutely make this joke.

jokes.text: Cajun style.
jokes.punchline: His repeated catchphrase punctuation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character tic. Carville adding 'Cajun style' to everything is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Cajun style.
jokes.punchline: Applying catchphrase to mundane action
jokes.explanation: Running gag that works through repetition. Shows Carville applies his philosophy to everything, even vending machines.

jokes.text: Your online fan club? That's me. I made Frank set that up as a punishment after he farted in my chair.
jokes.setup: Liz revealing her efforts to coddle Jenna
jokes.punchline: Fan club is fake, Frank forced to do it as punishment
jokes.explanation: Great revelation about the lengths Liz goes to. The chair farting detail makes Frank's punishment funnier.

jokes.text: And this award you just got? It's a cookie.
jokes.setup: Continuing to reveal Jenna's fake achievements
jokes.punchline: Award is literally just food
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the fake awards. A cookie is so pathetic it's brilliant. Great payoff.

jokes.text: I lie awake at night wondering what fresh hell tomorrow will bring.
jokes.setup: Liz's confession about managing Tracy and Jenna
jokes.punchline: Dorothy Parker reference about daily suffering
jokes.explanation: Literary reference that perfectly captures Liz's suffering. 'Fresh hell' is memorably dramatic while being relatable.

jokes.text: Dude, you got a frohawk, man. Do you think you're better than us?
jokes.setup: Toofer and Frank redirecting feud to Josh
jokes.punchline: Attacking Josh's haircut as elitist
jokes.explanation: Good scapegoating comedy. 'Frohawk' is a specific hairstyle that sounds pretentious to the writers.

jokes.text: I've got a new nickname for Josh. Dr. Haircut.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous insulting nickname
jokes.explanation: 'Dr. Haircut' is absurd enough to be memorable. Shows Frank's limited creativity in insults.

jokes.text: I don't know why it was so urgent you have it before noon.
jokes.punchline: Innocent confusion about romantic rendezvous timing
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocence about Jack's romantic schemes. His confusion about urgency is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I'm 43, and you have great hair.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if she trusts him
jokes.punchline: Shallow reasons for continuing relationship
jokes.explanation: Honest assessment of why she's dating Jack. The hair compliment is shallow but realistic relationship logic.

jokes.text: She's my liberal, hippy-dippy mama. My groovy chick. My old lady.
jokes.punchline: Corporate exec using 60s slang
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack overcompensation - using hippie slang to show his comfort with her politics. Hilariously awkward.

jokes.text: I gave to NPR last year.
jokes.punchline: Confession of liberal media support
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing corporate executives' secret liberal tendencies. NPR donation is perfect confession.

jokes.text: I'm gay. I'm black.
jokes.punchline: Identity revelations vs. political ones
jokes.explanation: Escalation peaks with identity confessions. Shows the toxic corporate environment where basic identity is shameful.

jokes.text: I murdered my wife.
jokes.punchline: Criminal confession tops political ones
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation peak - murder confession shows how supportive atmosphere enabled oversharing. Dark but perfectly timed.
